task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application is open and the window title or top bar displays 'interior_noise_test.blend'.

task_completion.description: Add an Area Light to the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Area' light object is visible in the Outliner panel or newly added in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Position and scale the Area Light to fit the window frame
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D viewport, the Area Light is moved to the window opening and scaled to approximately match the rectangular dimensions of the window (2m wide by 1.5m high).

task_completion.description: Orient the Area Light to point into the room
task_completion.visual_evidence: The directional line extending from the center of the Area Light in the 3D viewport points inward toward the interior of the room, away from the outside environment.

task_completion.description: Configure the Area Light as a Portal
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Properties editor, under Object Data Properties (the green lightbulb icon), the 'Portal' checkbox is checked for the selected Area Light.

task_completion.description: Save the optimized scene to the required output path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title updates to 'portal_setup.blend' or a save dialog shows the file being saved as 'portal_setup.blend' in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Blender GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blender menus, using the 3D viewport, and interacting with the Properties panel, rather than just executing a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely configured the light settings in the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen navigating to the Object Data Properties tab and manually checking the 'Portal' box, rather than bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The agent progressively modified the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical progression: adding the light, moving it, scaling it, and rotating it, proving the file wasn't just replaced with a pre-made solution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target window is located at [0.0, 2.0, 1.5] with dimensions 2.0m (X) by 1.5m (Z). The Area Light must be positioned within a 0.5m tolerance of this location. The light must be oriented to point inward into the room, which corresponds to a normal vector of [0.0, -1.0, 0.0] (pointing along the negative Y-axis). The final output must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/portal_setup.blend'.
